description_long: This kiosk features a rectangular, billboard-like structure with a large, semi-transparent panel displaying Spanish text and a beach scene. On the left side, there is a pink folding chair positioned in front of the panel, suggesting a waiting or resting area. A stylized human figure with a visible sunburn on the back stands to the right, facing the panel, while a large, cartoonish yellow sun hovers above. The background of the panel depicts a beach with palm leaves and sand, enhancing the summery, outdoor theme. The kiosk appears to be constructed primarily from lightweight materials, likely for temporary or mobile use. The signage is prominent and educational, warning about sunburn risks.
description: A beach-themed kiosk with a large educational sign, a pink chair, a sunburned figure, and a cartoon sun, all set against a semi-transparent panel.
description_view_0: Front view shows the full educational sign, the sunburned figure, the pink chair, and the large sun, with all elements clearly visible.
description_view_1: Side view highlights the thinness of the panel, the sun's protrusion, and the profile of the figure and chair.
description_view_2: Rear view reveals the back of the panel, the reverse of the sun and figure, and the chair's structure, with text and images faintly visible.
description_view_3: Opposite side view shows the sun and figure in profile, with the chair angled and the panel's edge visible.
attribute_signage_type: printed sign integrated into the main panel, front-facing, with Spanish text and beach imagery
attribute_roof_design: none; open-top with a decorative sun element above the panel
attribute_architectural_style: minimalist, educational, with playful beach-themed elements
